g.text: Why Causal Masking Enables Autoregressive Generation
Same constraint at train and inference
During training, mask prevents model from seeing future tokens even though they're in the batch
During inference, future tokens don't exist yet—mask ensures model never needed them
This train/test consistency is why autoregressive generation works
Position i can only attend to positions 0..i (itself and earlier)
Upper-triangular mask with -inf → softmax produces 0.0 for masked positions
Ensures model learns to generate without cheating by looking at future tokens
Same mask at training and inference = no distribution shift

text.tspan: Create causal mask for decoder self-attention.
Positions can only attend to themselves and earlier positions.
device: Device to create tensor on
mask: [1, 1, seq_len, seq_len] where True = masked (ignore)
# Add batch and head dimensions: [1, 1, seq, seq]
# exp(-inf) = 0, so masked positions contribute nothing
